Skip to contents
managelidar 0.1.5
  • Reference
    • How to use the functions

Create (or update) a STAC catalog

Source: R/stac_functions.R
stac_create_catalog.Rd

Creates a new root STAC catalog at the given path. This is the entry point of a STAC tree; collections are added underneath it with stac_add_collection().

Usage

stac_create_catalog(
  path,
  id,
  title = id,
  description = "STAC catalog",
  icon = NULL,
  copy = NULL
)

Arguments

path

Directory in which to create the catalog. catalog.json is written inside this directory.

id

Catalog ID. Ignored (with a warning) if a catalog already exists at path with a different ID, since IDs aren't renamed here.

title

Catalog title. Defaults to id for a new catalog; left unchanged on update unless explicitly passed.

description

Catalog description. Defaults to "STAC catalog" for a new catalog; left unchanged on update unless explicitly passed.

icon

A URL, or a path to a local image file, to set as the catalog's icon link (shown in headers/listings by STAC Browser and similar clients). See stac_set_icon() for details.

copy

Whether to copy icon into the catalog tree rather than reference it in place. See stac_set_icon() for the default behavior.

Value

Path to catalog.json (invisibly), for piping into stac_add_collection().

Details

If a catalog already exists at path, it is updated in place instead of being recreated: any argument you explicitly pass (title, description, icon) is applied; anything you don't pass is left untouched, so re-running this with just a new icon won't reset the title back to its default. Existing links (e.g. child links to collections added since) are preserved.

Examples

tempfile("stac-managelidar-") |>
  stac_create_catalog(id = "lidar_ni", title = "Sample Catalog")
#> ✔ Created catalog lidar_ni at /tmp/RtmpWTNJfp/stac-managelidar-215b6d21ab5e/catalog.json
